summary: Frontend-only validation of OmniLegal Chainlit console. All 14 checkpoints from the review request were exercised via Playwright against the public preview URL. Chainlit loads correctly; welcome renders with 'OmniLegal — Legal Research Console' + all persona descriptions + 'Indexed source passages: 1,878'. Default persona is Tourist with the compass icon at top-left. Persona dropdown lists all 4 personas (Tourist, Law Student, Researcher, Layman). Welcome message correctly updates when persona is switched (Law Student welcome says 'IRAC analysis with citations and case law'). Tourist query about Indian traveller + Russian police returned a real grounded answer in ~11s with 'Persona: Tourist · Sources retrieved: 5 · Engine: emergent' and a populated Sources panel ([S1]-[S5] with jurisdiction, doc_type, page number and quoted excerpts, including Malcolm Shaw pp.1943 and p.1119 and Motor Vehicles Act 1988). Mid-session persona switch (Tourist -> Law Student) worked; the follow-up jus cogens query returned a full IRAC answer (Issue / Rule / Application / Conclusion) in ~19s, Engine: emergent, 5 sources retrieved, with inline numeric citations grounded in the retrieved Shaw excerpts. No literal escape codes (\00B6, \00A7, \u00B6, \u00A7) anywhere in the rendered UI. Only console noise is a harmless 'application/* MIME' warning, no JS errors that block rendering.

integration_issues.issue: Welcome message + system prompt instruct the model to 'Cite with [S#] tags', but the generated body text for the jus cogens query used plain numeric markers like [1], [2], [3], [4] instead of [S1], [S2], [S3], [S4]. The Sources panel still renders [S#] correctly, but readers must mentally map [N] -> [S#]. Consider either (a) strengthening the system prompt to force [S#] in body text, or (b) accepting [N] as an alias in citation_verifier so the two numbering styles stay aligned.

integration_issues.flow: Tourist retrieval relevance for cross-border queries
integration_issues.issue: Tourist query 'I am Indian and stopped by traffic police in Russia' retrieved 5 sources but [S1] and [S2] were unrelated Indian Constitution passages about 'Services under the Union' and 'War and peace' schedule entries. Retrieval appears to key off the word 'Indian' rather than 'Russia/traffic/police'. The LLM sensibly ignored the noise and produced a solid answer, but the Sources panel weakens user trust. Consider boosting jurisdiction-aware reranking or filtering by doc_type for Tourist-mode queries.

issues: Only the selected persona chip (e.g. 'Tourist') is shown in the top-left; the full list of 4 personas is a hover/click dropdown. Discoverable, but a new user might not realise personas are switchable — consider a subtle caret + 'Switch persona' tooltip on first load.

action_items: Low priority: Align inline citation style in synthesized answers so the body uses [S#] consistently with the Sources panel (currently model emits [1]/[2]/[3] for Law Student IRAC answers).

Low priority: Improve retrieval relevance for Tourist cross-border queries — jurisdiction-aware reranking or doc_type filtering so that 'Russia traffic police' does not surface unrelated Indian constitutional schedule entries as the top sources.
critical_code_review_comments: Persona switching through Chainlit ChatProfiles correctly triggers a fresh @cl.on_chat_start per persona; state is not leaked between personas (verified: Tourist welcome -> Law Student welcome text completely replaced, icon changed from compass to graduation cap).

LLM chain is healthy: engine tag 'emergent' visible on both Tourist and Law Student responses, confirming Claude Haiku 4.5 via emergentintegrations is the active provider; no 'local_practical_fallback' fallback was ever triggered despite empty GEMINI_API_KEY.
Sources panel rendering is correct: each [S#] card shows citation / jurisdiction / doc_type / page range / quoted excerpt, matches the spec in the review request.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of critical checkpoints passed; 2 minor relevance/citation-style issues noted

test_credentials: N/A — Chainlit is unauthenticated in preview
seed_data_creation: None — used the pre-bootstrapped 1,878-chunk embedded Qdrant corpus.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Chainlit runs on supervisor entry 'chainlit' (python -u run_chainlit.py, port 3000). Default 'backend' and 'frontend' supervisor entries are intentionally FATAL — do not restart them. Public URL: https://0983ebb0-4ce5-4bcf-a65b-3ef78edafe82.preview.emergentagent.com/. Cold-start first query ~11-20 s, warm queries faster. Use textarea#chat-input for typed queries and press Enter to submit. Persona pill is at top-left of header (get_by_text('Tourist'/'Law Student'/'Researcher'/'Layman')). Welcome text key markers to look for: 'OmniLegal — Legal Research Console', 'Indexed source passages: 1,878'. Response key markers: 'Persona: <name> · Sources retrieved: <N> · Engine: emergent'. Researcher + Layman persona flows were NOT re-exercised in this iteration (already pre-validated by E1 per agent_to_agent_context_note) — if main agent wants extra coverage, repeat the same Playwright pattern with the other two personas.
rca of the issue: No blocking bugs found. Minor issues listed are cosmetic/relevance nudges, not crashes.
